title: Security Awareness Training Completion for BES Personnel

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-004-6 R1 Part 1.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-004-6 R1 Part 1.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.9: Security Awareness Training Completion for BES Personnel.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.9' running on index=hr sourcetype=lms:completion, bes_privileged_users.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| inputlookup bes_privileged_users.csv
| join type=left employee_id [
    search index=hr sourcetype=lms:completion course_id="CIP_SECURITY_AWARENESS" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(completed_at) as completed_at by employee_id
  ]
| eval completed_epoch=strptime(completed_at,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval stale=if(isnull(completed_epoch) OR completed_epoch<relative_time(now(),"-350d@d"),1,0)
| where stale=1
| table employee_id, role, completed_at
description: We list personnel with active BES access who have not completed the current awareness module so training evidence is current before audits.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes hr and sourcetypes lms:completion, lms:completion`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Sync LMS completions nightly. (2) Align `employee_id` with directory accounts. (3) Alert managers fourteen days before the annual due wave. (4) Export CSV for the training coordinator. Indexes required: `index=hr`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=lms:completion`, `sourcetype=lms:completion``; Lookups: `bes_privileged_users`, `bes_privileged_users.csv`.
visualization: Table (incomplete), Single value (percent complete), Column chart (by department). Key fields: `completed_at`, `completed_epoch`, `employee_id`, `role`, `stale`.
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, registered change tickets, and approved work can look like noncompliance in raw telemetry; confirm against the current change record before you escalate.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=hr` `sourcetype=lms:completion`, `bes_privileged_users.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Sync LMS completions nightly. (2) Align `employee_id` with directory accounts. (3) Alert managers fourteen days before the annual due wave. (4) Export CSV for the training coordinator.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup bes_privileged_users.csv
| join type=left employee_id [
    search index=hr sourcetype=lms:completion course_id="CIP_SECURITY_AWARENESS" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(completed_at) as completed_at by employee_id
  ]
| eval completed_epoch=strptime(completed_at,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval stale=if(isnull(completed_epoch) OR completed_epoch<relative_time(now(),"-350d@d"),1,0)
| where stale=1
| table employee_id, role, completed_at
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security Awareness Training Completion for BES Personnel (CIP-004-6 R1 Part 1.1)** — We list personnel with active BES access who have not completed the current awareness module so training evidence is current before audits.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=hr` `sourcetype=lms:completion`, `bes_privileged_users.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **completed_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **stale** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where stale=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: completed_at, completed_epoch, employee_id, role, stale.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP firewall log, Windows Security, or the asset register—whichever the search is built to mirror. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (incomplete), Single value (percent complete), Column chart (by department)
